title: ब्रह्मा सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री ब्रह्मा सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् (मुख्य श्लोक) ॥

ॐ ब्रह्मा विश्वकृद् विश्वधाता विश्वात्मा विश्वभावनः।
विश्वकर्मा विश्वधारी विश्वेशः विश्वसम्भवः॥ १॥

चतुर्मुखश्चतुर्बाहुश्चतुर्वेदश्चतुर्गतिः।
चतुरात्मा चतुर्भावश्चतुर्वर्णविधायकः॥ २॥

प्रजापतिः सृष्टिकर्ता विधाता धाता विधिः।
स्वयम्भूर्भूतभव्यश्च भवोद्भवनकारणम्॥ ३॥

हिरण्यगर्भो ब्रह्माण्डी कमलासनसंस्थितः।
पद्मभूः पद्मनाभाङ्गो पद्मवासो महात्ययः॥ ४॥

हंसवाहो मुक्तकेशो दण्डकमण्डलूद्भवः।
अक्षमालाधरो दक्षो दक्षयज्ञविधायकः॥ ५॥

सावित्रीपतिरागम्यो वेदगर्भो महायशाः।
आदिकविर्गिरां नाथो गायत्रीवल्लभो विभुः॥ ६॥

लोकपितामहो धीमान् सर्वलोकप्रजापतिः।
अजो नित्यो महातेजाः सर्वयज्ञफलप्रदः॥ ७॥

राजसो गुणसम्पन्नो महामायाविशारदः।
तपोनिधिस्तपोमूर्तिस्तपःसिद्धो महातपाः॥ ८॥

वेदान्तवेद्यो वेदात्मा वेदवाहनवल्लभः।
सर्वज्ञः सर्वदः शान्तः सर्वभूतहिते रतः॥ ९॥

कालकर्ता कालवेत्ता कालचक्रप्रवर्तकः।
सृष्टिस्थितिलयानां च कर्ता धर्ता हरो हरिः॥ १०॥

विरिञ्चिः कमलोद्भूतः पुराणपुरुषो विराट्।
स्वराडधीशो भूतादिर्भूतभव्यभवत्प्रभुः॥ ११॥

सहस्रपादः सहस्राक्षो सहस्रवदनो हरः।
ब्रह्माणं शरणं यामि सृष्टिकर्तारमीश्वरम्॥ १२॥

॥ इति श्री ब्रह्मा सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् (मुख्य अंश) सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Brahma Sahasranama Stotram (Mukhya Shloka) ||

Om Brahma Vishvakrid Vishvadhata Vishvatma Vishvabhavanah |
Vishvakarma Vishvadhari Vishveshah Vishvasambhavah || 1 ||

Chaturmukhash Chaturbahush Chaturvedash Chaturgatih |
Chaturatma Chaturbhavash Chaturvarnavidhayakah || 2 ||

Prajapatih Srishtikarta Vidhata Dhata Vidhih |
Svayambhur Bhutabhavyash Cha Bhavodbhavana Karanam || 3 ||

Hiranyagarbho Brahmandi Kamalasana Samsthitah |
Padmabhuh Padmanabhango Padmavaso Mahatyayah || 4 ||

Hamsavaho Muktakesho Dandakamandaludbhavah |
Akshamaladharo Daksho Dakshayajnavidhayakah || 5 ||

Savitripatir Agamyo Vedagarbho Mahayashah |
Adikavir Giram Natho Gayatrivallabho Vibhuh || 6 ||

Lokapitamaho Dhiman Sarvalokaprajapatih |
Ajo Nityo Mahatejah Sarvayajnaphalapradah || 7 ||

Rajaso Gunasampanno Mahamayavisharadah |
Taponidhis Tapomurtis Tapahsiddho Mahatapah || 8 ||

Vedantavedyo Vedatma Vedavahana Vallabhah |
Sarvajnah Sarvadah Shantah Sarvabhutahite Ratah || 9 ||

Kalakarta Kalavetta Kalachakrapravartakah |
Srishtisthitilayanam Cha Karta Dharta Haro Harih || 10 ||

Virinchih Kamalodbhutah Purana Purusho Virat |
Svarad Adhisho Bhutadir Bhutabhavyabhavat Prabhuh || 11 ||

Sahasrapadah Sahasrakso Sahasravadano Harah |
Brahmanam Sharanam Yami Srishtikartaram Ishvaram || 12 ||

|| Iti Shri Brahma Sahasranama Stotram (Mukhya Amsha) Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
ब्रह्मा, विश्वकृत, विश्वधाता, विश्वात्मा, विश्वभावन, विश्वकर्मा, विश्वधारी, विश्वेश, विश्वसम्भव — इन नामों से ब्रह्मा का स्मरण करें।

चतुर्मुख, चतुर्बाहु, चतुर्वेदी, प्रजापति, सृष्टिकर्ता, विधाता, स्वयम्भू, हिरण्यगर्भ।

कमलासन, पद्मभू, हंसवाहन, अक्षमालाधर, सावित्रीपति, आदिकवि, गायत्रीवल्लभ।

लोकपितामह, सर्वज्ञ, कालकर्ता, विरिञ्चि, पुराणपुरुष — इन सहस्र नामों से ब्रह्मदेव की स्तुति होती है।
benefits: Contains thousand names of Lord Brahma the Creator
Extremely rare Sahasranama dedicated to Brahma
Invokes all aspects of Brahma as Creator, Prajapati, Virinchi
Invokes supreme creative power and knowledge of all vedas
Ideal for Brahma Jayanti and Thursday worship
description: The Brahma Sahasranama Stotram, known in Sanskrit as ब्रह्मा सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्, is a sacred recitation of 1000 divine names in praise of Lord Brahma. This sacred composition is sourced from Brahma Purana and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to grant supreme creative power and knowledge of all Vedas as well as invoke all aspects of Brahma as Creator, Prajapati, Virinchi. Additionally, it is known to contain thousand names of Lord Brahma the Creator. It is especially recommended on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Brahma Jayanti and Guru Purnima for maximum spiritual benefit. Comprising 12 verses, it can be completed in approximately 8 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Brahma Sahasranama Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Brahma Sahasranama Stotram and where is it preserved?
seoFaqs.answer: The Brahma Sahasranama Stotram (ब्रह्मा सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्) is the sacred recitation of the thousand names of Lord Brahma — the four-faced Creator (Chaturmukha), Pitamaha (grandfather of all beings), Hiranyagarbha (the golden womb), and consort of Devi Saraswati / Gayatri / Savitri. The text is preserved in the Brahma Purana and is one of the very few extant Sahasranamas dedicated to Brahma — making it especially rare and precious in the Sahasranama corpus, which is otherwise dominated by Vishnu, Shiva, Devi, Lalita, Ganesha and Subrahmanya stotras. It opens with the names Brahma, Vishvakrit, Vishvadhata, Vishvatma, Vishvabhavana, Vishvakarma, declaring Brahma as the maker, sustainer, soul and contemplator of the entire universe. (Sources: Brahma Purana; classical Smarta tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is a Sahasranama for Brahma so rare?
seoFaqs.answer: Although Lord Brahma is the first member of the Trimurti and the Creator of all beings, public worship of Brahma in Sanatana Dharma is almost entirely limited to the famous Brahma temple at Pushkar in Rajasthan and a handful of other shrines — a phenomenon traditionally explained by various puranic narratives involving the Brahma-Pulastya episode, the curse of Sage Bhrigu, and the cosmic principle that the Creator's work is complete after creation. As a consequence, dedicated Brahma stotras and a complete Brahma Sahasranama are extremely rare in everyday parayana, while Vishnu Sahasranama and Lalita Sahasranama are recited in millions of homes daily. Devotees who do worship Brahma — especially Vedic ritualists, Smarta brahmins and Pushkar pilgrims — therefore treasure this Sahasranama as an irreplaceable jewel of the Brahma Purana corpus. (Sources: Brahma Purana; Padma Purana Pushkar Mahatmya.)

seoFaqs.question: What names of Brahma occur in the opening verses?
seoFaqs.answer: The opening twelve verses of the Brahma Sahasranama compress the entire theology of the Creator into a cascade of names. Among them: Vishvakrit (maker of the universe), Vishvadhata (supporter of the universe), Vishvatma (soul of the universe), Chaturmukha (four-faced), Chaturbahu (four-armed), Chaturveda (knower of the four Vedas), Prajapati (lord of all beings), Srishtikarta (creator), Vidhata (ordainer), Svayambhu (self-born), Hiranyagarbha (the golden cosmic womb), Kamalasana (seated on the lotus), Padmabhu (born of the lotus), Hamsavaha (whose vehicle is the swan), Akshamaladhara (bearer of the rosary), Savitripati (consort of Savitri), Vedagarbha (whose womb is the Veda), Adikavi (the first poet — author of the Vedas), Giram Natha (lord of speech), Gayatrivallabha (beloved of Gayatri), Lokapitamaha (grandfather of all worlds), Virinchi and Purana Purusha. (Sources: Brahma Sahasranama Stotram opening dvadasha shlokas.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Brahma called Adikavi and Vedagarbha?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 6 of the Brahma Sahasranama hails Brahma as Adikavi (आदिकवि) — the first poet of the cosmos — and Vedagarbha (वेदगर्भ) — He whose womb contains the Vedas. According to the Veda-srishti narrative preserved across the Puranas, at the dawn of every kalpa Lord Brahma awakens from his cosmic meditation and the four Vedas — Rig, Yajur, Sama and Atharva — issue forth from his four mouths in the four cardinal directions. He is therefore the original revealer of all Vedic knowledge, the source of the Gayatri Mantra (his consort and shakti), and the primal author of all sacred speech (Giram Natha). When devotees recite this Sahasranama with shraddha, they are saluting the very fountainhead of all scripture, mantra and learning. (Sources: Brahma Purana; Manusmriti chapter 1.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Brahma Sahasranama be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Brahma Sahasranama is most powerfully recited on Thursdays (Guruvar) — the day of Brihaspati and Brahma; on Brahma Jayanti (traditionally observed on Kartik Purnima at Pushkar, when the Pushkar Mela also concludes); on every Purnima (full moon); on Vasant Panchami (when Saraswati, Brahma's consort, is worshipped); and during Guru Purnima (Ashadha Purnima). Pilgrims to Pushkar recite the Sahasranama after their snana in the Pushkar Sarovar before offering puja at the Brahma Mandir. The recitation should be done after bath, facing east, before a clean asana, with a small lamp, ideally accompanied by Gayatri japa. Vedic scholars and brahmacharis recite it to invoke pure knowledge before Vedic study. (Sources: Pushkar Mahatmya; Brahma upasana paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting the Brahma Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional Smarta acharyas attribute the following benefits to sincere recitation of the Brahma Sahasranama: invocation of the supreme creative power of Hiranyagarbha for new beginnings, projects, ventures and creative work; awakening of the buddhi to grasp the four Vedas and all branches of knowledge; cultivation of the Vedic sound-discipline that flows from Adikavi himself; the merit of all yajnas (sarva-yajna-phala-pradah) since Brahma is the lord of yajna; deepening of one's relationship with Gayatri and Savitri; success in scholarly, literary and academic pursuits; auspiciousness for marriage (since Brahma is the cosmic priest of every wedding); and the ultimate phala of moksha through realisation of Brahman, of which Brahma is the saguna manifestation. (Sources: Brahma Purana phalashruti; Smarta tradition.)
